name: Ascents to the Sierra de Ávila and Villanueva — circular from Peñaranda de Bracamonte
name_original: Subidas a la Sierra de Ávila y Villanueva — circular desde Peñaranda de Bracamonte
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Peñaranda De Bracamonte traverse a varied landscape, from the flatter plains surrounding local water bodies to the more challenging ascents into the Sierra de Ávila. The region features open countryside with occasional hills and passes, offering diverse terrain for road cyclists. Elevation changes can be significant on some routes, providing opportunities for sustained climbs and descents. Best road cycling routes around Peñaranda De Bracamonte The most popular road cycling route is Ascents to the Sierra de Ávila and Villanueva — circular from Peñaranda de Bracamonte, a 73.1 miles (117.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Ávila. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peñaranda Loop: Azud de Riolobos and Zorita de la Frontera, a moderate 37.1 miles (59.6 km) path. This route offers a more undulating profile, passing by the Azud de Riolobos. Local road cyclists also love the Fuente del Oro loop from Peñaranda de Bracamonte, a 27.0 miles (43.4 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Road cycling around Peñaranda De Bracamonte is defined by open plains, rolling hills, and challenging mountain passes, with options for both beginner road bike routes near Peñaranda De Bracamonte and advanced road bike trails Peñaranda De Bracamonte. The routes in Peñaranda De Bracamonte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñaranda De Bracamonte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: There are over 8 road cycling routes documented on komoot around Peñaranda De Bracamonte, offering a range of distances and difficulties for all cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: The region offers varied terrain, from flatter plains and rolling hills around local water bodies to more challenging ascents into the Sierra de Ávila. You'll find open countryside with opportunities for sustained climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes. For a pleasant ride through open countryside, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39836196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Oro loop from Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27.0 miles (43.4 km) long.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: For a more demanding ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063128286/ascents-to-the-sierra-de-avila-and-villanueva-circular-from-penaranda-de-bracamonte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascents to the Sierra de Ávila and Villanueva — circular from Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This difficult route covers 73.1 miles (117.6 km) with significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Ávila.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes or loops in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027043016/penaranda-loop-azud-de-riolobos-and-zorita-de-la-frontera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda Loop: Azud de Riolobos and Zorita de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 37.1 miles (59.6 km) path that passes by the Azud de Riolobos.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can see while road cycling?
A: Along the routes, you can encounter various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027043016/penaranda-loop-azud-de-riolobos-and-zorita-de-la-frontera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riolobos Weir&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel of Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt; and the historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zorita de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: The road cycling routes in Peñaranda De Bracamonte are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, from open plains to challenging mountain passes, offering something for every skill level.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that explore the Sierra de Ávila?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063128286/ascents-to-the-sierra-de-avila-and-villanueva-circular-from-penaranda-de-bracamonte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascents to the Sierra de Ávila and Villanueva — circular from Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you through the Sierra de Ávila, providing significant climbs and panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Peñaranda De Bracamonte?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, regions with varied terrain like Peñaranda De Bracamonte typically offer pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder weather, especially in higher elevations like the Sierra de Ávila.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31471587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Oro – Las Fuentes Pass loop from Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option, covering over 63 miles (102 km) with substantial elevation gain.

Q: Can I find routes that combine flat sections with some undulating terrain?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027043016/penaranda-loop-azud-de-riolobos-and-zorita-de-la-frontera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda Loop: Azud de Riolobos and Zorita de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt; offer a more undulating profile, providing a mix of flatter sections and gentle climbs, perfect for a varied ride.
tips: Beautiful church located in the town of Mancera de Arriba. This one, made of stone from the area, has flagged the core since a long time ago.
Mountain pass located in the center of the province of Ávila and which is 1,499 meters above sea level and connects the Amblés valley with San Juan del Olmo and the Muñico valley.

The relief of the municipality is formed by two mountainous areas separated by the Amblés valley, through which the Adaja river runs, which is born in the territory. The northern area is called Sierra de Villanueva, a branch of the Sierra de Ávila.
